brief.topic: Prompt caching in large language models
brief.goal: Help backend engineers understand when and how to use prompt caching to cut costs and latency
brief.notes: Target audience already knows what LLMs are but hasn't used caching APIs

profile.name: Habr
profile.audience: Russian-speaking backend engineers with 3–10 years of experience

clarifications.question: Which LLM providers should the article cover — Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, or all three?
clarifications.answer: Focus on Anthropic (Claude) with brief mentions of OpenAI for comparison

clarifications.question: Should the article include concrete cost-calculation examples with real token prices?
clarifications.answer: Yes, include a worked example with actual API pricing

angles.title: The 90% Cost Cut: Prompt Caching Under the Hood
angles.rationale: Engineers want to understand the mechanism before trusting it in production; a technical deep-dive with benchmarks builds that confidence.

angles.title: When to Cache and When Not To: A Decision Guide
angles.rationale: A practical decision-tree article lets readers immediately apply the knowledge to their own projects without reading theory first.

angles.title: From 500ms to 50ms: Refactoring a RAG Pipeline with Caching
angles.rationale: A concrete before/after case study with real numbers resonates strongly with engineers who evaluate ideas by seeing them in practice.

snapshot.recommendationReason: The technical deep-dive angle best matches the target audience's desire to understand the mechanism before adopting it in production.
